column3: A document that records information about one or more entities. Can contain free text, incorporate images, and be composed of ordered parts. Parts are themselves simple_document instances, allowing figures, sections, and composite reports to be modelled within the same structure. Document type distinguishes structural role (e.g. figure, working note, report section); domain type distinguishes subject matter (e.g. x-radiography, provenance).
Document Note: Simple Document Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Simple Document Model Overview
column3: This model defines a reusable document entity that can represent anything from a single figure caption to a multi-section technical report. A document can incorporate images directly and can be composed of ordered part-documents. Contribution provenance is captured via the document_contribution model. The model is intentionally generic; source-specific typing and content are applied through the domain type and document type controlled lists.
The entity or entities this document is about. Can be a heritage object, sample, examination event, or any other CRM entity. One document may document multiple entities.
The creation event for this document.
TBC.
An image directly incorporated into this document. For figure-type documents this is the primary content. For other document types images may be incorporated alongside text.

An ordered part of this document. Parts are themselves simple_document instances. A figure part incorporates an image and carries a caption as its document text. Parts may have their own creation provenance and type.
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.
The display label or title of the document.
The textual content of the document. For figure parts this carries the caption. May be absent for purely structural container documents.
The structural role of this document. Selected from a controlled list: working_note, report_section, figure, composite_report, catalogue_entry.

The subject domain of this document. Selected from a controlled list: e.g. x-radiography, infrared_reflectography, entry_text, provenance, condition, treatment, support, paint_layers, surface_coatings, bibliography, organic_analysis, cross_section.

A dimension node representing the ordinal position of this part within its parent document. The numeric value is not stored directly on this node; a separate literal structure would be required to record it. The parent document context is captured via the associated E13 Attribute Assignment using P16.

column3: E55: Ordinal Position Type
E55: Ordering Property Type
A type node indicating the kind of dimension recorded by this E54 node. Fixed value: "ordinal position". Distinguishes this dimension from physical or temporal measurements.

A type node indicating the nature of this attribute assignment. Fixed value: "order within document". Indicates that the E13 Attribute Assignment is recording the ordinal position of a part within a specific parent document.
